statement: name a country that is at least 20,000 sq km and has a population over 5,000,000.

statement: name a geopolitical entity that is at least 20,000 sq km and has a population over 5,000,000, yet must simultaneously be smaller than 10,000 sq km and host fewer than 1,000,000 people.
table_text: al itthad al sakandary - el-olympi - haras el hodood
al-ahly - al mokawloon - enppi - el-jaish - el-shorta - itesalat

chain.thought:
Select relevant rows.
The question is asking for a geopolitical entity that meets two contradictory conditions simultaneously, which is impossible. Therefore, there is no row in the table that can answer this question.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
a geopolitical entity -> name
at least 20,000 sq km -> area (km²)
a population over 5,000,000 -> population (2006)
smaller than 10,000 sq km -> area (km²)
fewer than 1,000,000 people -> population (2006)
column value of the question link to columns:
20,000 -> area (km²)
5,000,000 -> population (2006)
10,000 -> area (km²)
1,000,000 -> population (2006)
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
None

The question is asking to name a geopolitical entity that meets specific criteria for both area and population. To find the correct answer, we need to sort the data based on the "area (km²)" column in the order of "small to large" using the f_sort_column(area (km²)) operation. Subsequently, we should also sort the "population (2006)" column in the order of "small to large" using the f_sort_column(population (2006)) operation. This will help us identify the entity that satisfies the given conditions of area and population.

The question is asking to identify a geopolitical entity that meets two contradictory sets of conditions simultaneously. This makes it impossible since an entity cannot be both larger than 20,000 sq km and smaller than 10,000 sq km at the same time, nor can it have a population over 5,000,000 and fewer than 1,000,000 people simultaneously. We need to examine the "area (km²)" and "population (2006)" columns to confirm no such entity exists in this dataset.

*/

Explanation: The question aims to identify the specific Jeep Grand Cherokee model that has the OM647 diesel i5 engine and also determine which one among them has the third lowest displacement value.

If the table only needs a few columns to answer the question, we use f_select_column() to select these columns for it. For example,

If the question is about items with the same value and the number of these items, we use f_group_column() to group the items. For example,

If the question is about the order of items in a column, we use f_sort_column() to sort the items. For example,
